text: Handling deadlines effectively is a crucial skill in both academic and professional settings. Good time management helps you plan your tasks and avoid last-minute stress. Start by breaking large projects into smaller steps, and set mini-deadlines for each one. Prioritization is equally important—identify which tasks are most urgent or carry the highest impact, and tackle those first. Scope management means being realistic about what you can achieve; don’t agree to do more than your time allows. Finally, communication is key. If you anticipate a delay, let your manager or team know early and suggest adjustments. This builds trust and prevents surprises. With these strategies, you can meet deadlines calmly and reliably.
